The quote is paraphrasing the first chapter of The Interpretation of Dreams by Sigmund Freud (1900) https://i.imgur.com/Sjam7Cj.jpg
item.dc:creator: ChalupBatmanSugarBottomTheThird

item.title: The Vaults of Fallout
item.description:
With the success of Amazon's Fallout series, I figured it would be a good time to revisit all of the known Vaults throughout the series. This includes all Vaults from Fallout, Fallout 2, Fallout 3, Fallout: New Vegas, Fallout 4, Fallout 76, and the show (so, spoilers for that), which has been stated to be canon within the world of the games. This does not include non-canon Vaults like the ones in the Fallout Shelter mobile game, Fallout Tactics, and Fallout: Brotherhood of Steel. I also did not include Vaults that were mentioned in-game, but with no known location or number attached, or the ones from the Fallout board game. However, unnumbered or unofficial Vaults that do appear in games are included.
What are Vaults? Vaults are underground shelters within the Fallout universe that are capable of safely housing and sustaining up to one thousand people in case of a nuclear war. That is the stated purpose, anyway. Their true purpose was to serve as social experiments to suggest how to rebuild humanity once the surface was safe to repopulate, with seventeen Vaults with no actual experiment being conducted to serve as a control group. in 2054, as tensions with China began to flare up, the United States government began Project Safehouse to ensure humanity's survival, with Vault-Tec ultimately winning the contract and immediately relocating their headquarters from Los Angeles to Washington DC and beginning construction on more than one hundred Vaults across the country.
Vault 3 Location: Las Vegas, Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Control. A water leak some time in the 23rd century forced the residents to open the door and begin trading with the residents of the Mojave Wasteland. It was eventually infiltrated by a gang of drug addicts known as the Fiends, who killed the original inhabitants and claimed the Vault for themselves.
Vault 4 Location: Los Angeles, California Appearance: the show Purpose: To see how a society governed by scientists with no limits on control or decisions would behave. This led to the scientists attempting to hybridize humans with radiation-resistant wildlife. Ultimately, the subjects rebelled and killed the scientists, including the Overseer and his wife being eaten alive. The remaining residents decided to remain in the Vault, establishing a democracy within the Vault, accepting refugees from the wasteland, and attempting to undo the mutations caused by their original Overseer.
Vault 8 Location: Northwestern Nevada Appearance: Fallout 2 Purpose: Control. The Vault was stocked with a Garden of Eden Creation Kit (GECK), terraforming tools to help make the surface inhabitable. It also received hundreds of water purifier chips that were meant for Vault 13, which received an extra GECK that was meant for Vault 8. Fourteen years after the bombs fell, an "all clear" message was received, and the residents began recolonizing the wasteland, establishing what would become Vault City outside the door.
Vault 11 Location: Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Test residents willingness to sacrifice few for the good of the majority, using false threats of collective punishment. The sacrifices were initially chosen democratically, but this practice was ultimately abolished, which led to an attempted coup and the deaths of most residents.
Vault 12 Location: Bakersfield, California Appearance: Fallout Purpose: Study of the effects of gradual radiation exposure. The door was designed to not properly seal, which let radiation in. The inhabitants became Ghouls, and established the city of Necropolis.
Vault 13 Location: California Appearance: Fallout, Fallout 2 Purpose: Control. Residents chose to continue living in isolation until 2161, when a broken water purifier chip caused the Overseer to send residents out to find replacements. Once the player has completed their mission and returned, they are then sent to find the source of the Ghouls and other mutants that could be a threat to the Vault. After completing this mission, The Vault Dweller was then banished, lest other residents attempt to make it on their own in the wasteland, and unknowingly abandon the experiment. Some residents were disgusted by this betrayal, and left with The Vault Dweller. Those that remained arrested the Overseer, held a trial, and sentenced him to death, while abolishing the position of Overseer. They then commissioned the Brotherhood of Steel to install a mainframe computer to maintain the Vault's functions. Eighty years later, The Vault Dweller's grandchild, The Chosen One, is tasked with acquiring Vault 13's GECK to save their village of Arroyo from a historic drought. Earlier that year, the Vault's residents received the "all-clear" message that was sent by The Enclave, the Fascist remnants of the US government. three dwellers were killed, and the rest sent to an oil rig. They then sent in a pack of intelligent Deathclaws to cover up the operation and maintain The Enclave's control of the Vault.
Vault 15 Location: Southern California Appearance: Fallout, Fallout 2 Purpose: A large population with diverse ethnic and religious backgrounds. The Vault was not opened after the planned fifty years, and with no population control plan in place, it grew overcrowded, and residents forced their way out. One group of residents armed with the Vault's GECK established the city of Shady Sands, which served as the capital of the New California Republic until its destruction via nuclear bomb some time after 2281. Other residents of Vault 15 gathered into three distinct Raider gangs: The Khans, Jackals, and Vipers, who spent years terrorizing the NCR. Large parts of the Vault collapsed due to earthquakes, but squatters began living in the remains in 2241, under the protection of the New Khans, and refused to give entry to the NCR, until The Chosen One is able to broker a deal to kill the New Khans' leader in exchange for the squatters joining the NCR.
Vault 17 Location: West Coast Appearance: Mentioned in Fallout: New Vegas, Vault 17 Jumpsuit appears as an unused item in the data of Fallout 4 Purpose: Unknown. Lily Bowen and her grandchildren are the only known inhabitants. They lived in the Vault until Lily was 75, and all residents were kidnapped by The Master's army.
Vault 19 Location: Mojave Desert, Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Artificially induce paranoia among two completely isolated groups of residents. Subjected to subliminal messaging and faked acts of sabotage, many residents developed symptoms of psychosis. The ultimate fate of the residents is unknown. Vault eventually occupied by the gang known as the Powder Gangers.
Vault 21 Location: Las Vegas, Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: All residents were gambling addicts, and all conflicts among residents were solved by games of chance. Was one of the few non-control Vaults to not fail, as it still had a stable population until Mr. House won ownership of the Vault in a game of blackjack in 2274, and converted it into a hotel.
Vault 22 Loaction: Mojave Desert, Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Research facility creating genetically modified crops and flora. A modified fungus ultimately killed the researchers living in the Vault.
Vault 29 Location: West Coast Appearance: Mentioned in Fallout and Fallout 76 Purpose: All residents were children of affluent families. No one older than fifteen was allowed in before the door was sealed. The non-canon Fallout Bible states that the Ghoul known as Harold was a resident of this Vault.
Vaults 31, 32, & 33 Location: Santa Monica: California Appearance: the show Purpose: Three separate but interconnected Vaults, ostensibly to serve as extra security against threats, but also support each other in times of crisis. Secretly, it was to ensure the survival of Vault-Tec's corporate ideals. Several Vault-Tec managers are kept in cryogenic stasis in Vault 31, with ones periodically thawed to serve as Overseers of Vaults 32 and 33. Residents of Vault 32 eventually learned the truth and revolted, with most residents dying of infighting, suicide, or starvation. In 2294, a group of Raiders led by Lee Moldaver gain entry to Vault 32, and use that Vault's equipment to also infiltrate Vault 33 and kidnap current Overseer Hank MacLean, leaving his daughter Lucy to go after them.
Vault 34 Location: Mojave Desert, Nevada Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Residents were given access to an armory overstocked with weapons and ammunition with no security measures. Once the armory was locked manually, a faction of residents left, forming the group known as the Boomers. Remaining residents either died or were Ghoulified buy a reactor leak.
Vault 51 Location: Northern West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Residents were overseen by an AI named ZAX 1.3c, who was tasked with selecting the ideal Overseer from the Vault's fifty residents. It began manufacturing crises to test leadership qualities, which then escalated into lethal threats, and then manipulating the residents into killing each other.
Vault 63 Location: Southwest of Lewisburg, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Unknown. The Vault is currently completely inaccessible. Its purpose is expected to be revealed in the game's Skyline Valley update, planned for June of 2024.
Vault 75 Location: Malden, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: Eugenics experiments. Upon entering the Vault, children were separated from their parents, who were then executed. The children were brainwashed into fearing the Wasteland, while being trained to be strong enough to survive the "Uptopland". Upon reaching the age of eighteen, the children would "graduate", with the strong ones being killed and their organs and genetic material harvested for the next generation, the intelligent and obedient ones recruited to help the scientists, and those who did not meet the standards of the Overseer were executed, with their bodies incinerated. Eventually, the subjects were able to rebel, and leave the Vault, which was then occupied by the gang known as the Gunners.
Vault 76 Loacation: North of Flatwoods, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Control. The official Vault of America's tricentennial celebration, residents included high-ranking military members, students of Vault-Tec University, and a former White House Chief of Staff. It was among the first Vaults to open after the Great War, with the occasion celebrated by the residents as Reclamation Day. At some point before the Great War, Vault-Tec's assistant CEO was inspecting Vault 76's construction when he was abducted by aliens.
Vault 77 Location: Unknown Appearance: Penny Arcade's Fallout 3 tie-in comic. Purpose: Study of extreme isolation. The Vault's only resident was one man, left with a crate full of puppets. An imagined scenario with the puppets involves the man using a Vault Boy puppet to kill the king. He chooses to leave the Vault with the Vault Boy puppet, and eventually uses it to kill a group of slavers. A Vault 77 Jumpsuit can be found in Fallout 3, in the slaver settlement of Paradise Falls, along with a holotape warning to burn the suit, lest the Puppet Man return to take his revenge.
Vault 79 Location: North of Dolly Sands Wilderness, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: A Secret Service facility meant to house the United States' gold reserves. A reactor leak killed or Ghoulified most residents. Survivors were rescued in 2103 by residents of Vault 76 during a heist for the gold.
Vault 81 Location: Boston, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: A secret sector of the Vault run by scientists were to conduct experiments on the unwitting population in an effort to find a universal cure for all diseases. The Overseer objected and sabotaged the experiment, sealing the scientists in their sector with no means of escaping or contacting the outside world. The scientists experimented on Mole Rats, but they all eventually died. The residents, unaware of any experiments, continued their existence until 2277, when the Overseer opened the door to begin trading with the outside world. Most residents, however, had an isolationist or xenophobic view of those not born in the Vault.
Vault 87 Location: Northeastern Virginia Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Study human exposure to the Forced Evolutionary Virus. Residents were transformed into Super Mutants, who took control of the Vault and escaped into the Wasteland.
Vault 88 Location: Northwest of Quincy, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: Testing facility for experimental productivity-boosting equipment to be used in other Vaults. The Vault was still under construction when the bombs fell, which led to its only occupant, the Overseer, Valery Barstow to become Ghoulified. in 2287, The Sole Survivor is enlisted by Barstow to help complete the Vault's construction and fulfill its purpose
Vault 92 Location: Olney, Maryland Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Attempt to induce aggression via white noise exposure. All residents were musicians driven to insanity by the white noise, who then killed each other. It is infested by Bloatflies and Mirelurks by the time the Lone Wanderer explores the Vault to find a Stradivarius violin within its depths.
Vault 94 Location: North of Dolly Sands Wilderness, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Test the viability of pacifistic ideals in a post-apocalyptic setting. All residents were members of a nonviolent religious order except for the Overseer, who was an employee of Vault-Tec. So as to not interfere with the experiment, he was given a false identity as a maintenance engineer and given orders to minimize contact with Vault residents. The residents ruled themselves with a democratic council, and were given communal living spaces instead of private quarters. The Vault was also given a large supply of highly-valuable resources, but no weapons, armor, or other defenses, and encouraged to open the door to Wastelanders that would likely be armed and desperate. The Overseer eventually revealed his role and orders after ingratiating himself into the community, and falling in love with the daughter of the order's leader. The residents sent ambassador parties into the Wasteland to invite all survivors they could to the Vault. The first group to arrive were suspicious of their hospitality, believing it to be some kind of trap. They killed the council, and shot the GECK, thinking it was some kind of mind control device. This caused a meltdown of the GECK's fusion generator, leading to a large nuclear explosion which killed all residents and rapidly intensified the area's swampy environment and mutated the dense vegetation.
Vault 95 Location: Southeast of Natick, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: All residents were drug addicts lured by the potential of experimental rehab treatments, only to then be informed that the Vault had a large stash of drugs once the treatments were over. Residents initially overcame their addictions, but quickly descended into violence when they were informed of the stash. Eventually occupied by the Gunners.
Vault 96 Location: South of Spruce Knob Campground, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Staffed by just five individuals tasked with studying mutated wildlife to develop anti-mutation countermeasures. Automated security measures threatened to kill any researcher who did not meet their quotas, due to the secretive and unethical nature of their research. The researchers attempted to sabotage to security systems and escape, but failed and were killed. Test subjects eventually escaped.
Vault 101 Location: West of Springvale, Virginia Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Overseer granted unlimited authority, with the Vault intended to remain sealed indefinitely. However, the door has been opened a few times over the years, most notably in 2259 to let in a scientist named James and his child, who would eventually become The Lone Wanderer, in exchange for James agreeing to become the Vault's doctor. In 2277, James left Vault 101, leaving his child to wander the wasteland in search of him. This leads to a schism between the Vault's residents with half, led by the current Overseer, being in favor of remaining isolated, and the other half, led by the Overseer's daughter, being in favor of opening the Vault and trading with the nearby settlement of Megaton.
Vault 106 Location: Northeastern Virginia Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Psychotropic drugs were pumped into the air filtration system. The residents quickly erupted into violence. Not much is known about their fate, but the drugs were potent enough to still linger in the air over two hundred years later.
Vault 108 Location: North of Washington DC Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Studying the effects of leadership conflicts. The first Overseer was terminally ill before the Great War, the power systems were designed to fail after twenty years, no entertainment was provided, and the armory with stocked with triple the usual amount of weapons and ammunition. The Vault also had a cloning facility. By 2277, the only residents are all clones of the same man, named Gary, who are extremely hostile, and only capable of saying their own name.
Vault 111 Location: Northwest of Concord, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: Study the long-term effects of cryogenic stasis. After the predetermined period of time passed without receiving the "all clear" signal, and facing dwindling supplies, Vault Security mutineed against the Overseer and abandoned the Vault. in 2227 an organization known as The Institute learned of the Vault's location and that there was an infant frozen inside who had not been exposed to radiation. The Institute infiltrate the Vault and the infant, Shaun, is ripped from the arms of the parent that was holding him, who is then murdered, while the other parent could only watch on in horror. Sixty years later, the remaining parent thaws out, only to discover that they are The Sole Survivor of their Vault, unaware of how much time has passed since Shaun's abduction, and set out to find him.
Vault 112 Location: Southwest of Fort Bannister, Virginia Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: Residents are placed in a virtual reality simulation controlled by the Overseer, Stanislaus Braun, creator of the GECKs. Braun sadistically toys with the residents, before killing the simulated versions of them and wiping their memories, repeating this process over a span of more than two hundred years.
Vault 114 Location: Boston, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: Residents would have been wealthy people forced to live in cramped squalor, under the watch of an incompetent Overseer. Construction was never finished, and the site was eventually taken over by a gangster named Tony Malone and his crew, the Triggermen.
Vault 118 Location: Mount Desert Island, Maine Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: Ten wealthy Overseers would have been given total authority over three hundred impoverished people. Construction of the "lower class" section was never completed, and residents of the "luxury" section eventually had their brains transferred into robotic bodies in an attempt to achieve some kind of immortality.
Demonstration Vault Location: Los Angeles, California Appearance: Fallout Appearance: A demonstration Vault beneath Vault-Tec's original headquarters built in an ultimately successful attempt to get the contract for the US government's Project Safehouse. Later occupied the The Master and their army.
Securitron Vault Location: Fortification Hill, Arizona Appearance: Fallout: New Vegas Purpose: Mr. House had this built before the Great War to securely store a massive number of Securitron robots, and the facilities necessary to create even more. It can only be opened with the Platinum Chip.
"Vault of Tomorrow" Location: Museum of Technology, Washington DC Appearance: Fallout 3 Purpose: A publicity stunt by Vault-Tec to encourage people to volunteer to live in the Vaults.
Vault-Tec: Among the Stars Location: Nuka World, Massachusetts Appearance: Fallout 4 Purpose: A publicity stunt by Vault-Tec to convince people how their technologies could be used in space exploration and colonization.
Makeshift Vault Location: Northwest of Grafton, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Survival. Constructed by former Vault-Tec employees using stolen equipment. The constructors died some time after the Great War, where it was then occupied by a Raider group known as the War Party, who used it to store their stockpile of stolen Hellstorm missile launchers. Vault 76 residents then aid the Brotherhood of Steel drive the Raiders away.
Vault-Tec University Simulation Vault Location: Morgantown, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Assist Vault-Tec trainees in learning about how Vaults function. A class was actually inside when the bombs fell, but died within days, as it was not equipped with food or water.
Whitespring Congressional Bunker Location: The Whitespring Resort, West Virginia Appearance: Fallout 76 Purpose: Continuation of US governmental function. Meant to house members of Congress, the US President, and the President's Cabinet in the event of nuclear war. After the bombs fell. Everyone inside who was not a member of the Fascistic Enclave faction was rounded up and executed. Following the chain of succession, this left Secretary of Agriculture Thomas Eckhart as the new President, who immediately began his efforts to eradicate Communism.

item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: I ADMIT IT OKAY?!!
item.description: This is how it is explaining to people what I do for a living Ahahaha!!! I’m trying to branch out okay! It’s really hard trying to explain, whether it be to strangers I meet, family, new friends etc…it’s really hard. On every date I went on I didn’t have a problem telling people though, cuz I felt it better for them to know straight away ahahaha. But yeah no I really love drawing pinup art. I decided I really enjoyed making short form comics aswell, hence why my comic account was born. It’s still early days, I’m trying to juggle commissions, patreon and my new comics…I’m always interested in new projects. I definitely don’t have the stamina of other artists My comic instagram and p@treon https://www.instagram.com/miaminicomics https://www.patreon.com/miaminicomics b00ba accounts: Extra https://www.patreon.com/miaormoa Dono and commissions: https://ko-fi.com/miagabriella Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/miaormoa
item.dc:creator: miaormoa

item.title: (1 year difference) Even at my highest weight, I refused to wear black tents to conceal my body. I do like that I’m getting smaller though.
item.description: I don’t look thin by any means but I’m working on it. ETA: Imgur people, you are way too kind and so encouraging and just amazing. It’s humbling. Thank you for taking time out of your busy schedule to drop by. I truly, truly appreciate it.
item.dc:creator: ssmc

item.title: It's about time
item.description: I'd like to preface that I'm starting on Testosterone. It took me some time to realize what was going on with myself because there's really not much out there about FtM compared to MtF, if y'all could share some masc stuff I'd appreciate it! FP edit: oh wow, this was... A lot more than I expected! Thank you all so much for the well wishes and open arms!!

item.dc:creator: KingreX32

item.title: A lot going on here.... (electrician's horror)
item.description: EDIT: this is an on-going story about my electricity box.At no point am I asking for money, I have the money! Stop downvoting. Unless ya hate electricity box messes and I ruined yer day. Sorry if that is the case. ---------- This is a electricity supply box (in Greece), one that the user is not allowed to touch, open or -off course- tamper with inside. I have tri-phase power (not all houses have that, I have no idea why this is the case since this is a smol house) Greece outside the 3 big cities is not an organised land. The islands are...bit....30 year in the past. Its still bribing, who-you-know etc Per contract with the company I am charged for any and all work, if this shit needs maintenance. This shit that I am NOT ALLOWED to open (it has a seal that the crows steal), have my private electrician look at or anything else. The seal costs 15euro after taxes and these nice molten beauties about 70 euro after taxes. I have called [I am forced to or I will be fined for 'not calling services'] their designated electricity maintenance team DEDDYE through the system that records your call AND made an additional online application, with photos and clear instructions that I will need paperwork, proof of work,in front of two witnesses (this is Greece, the bamboozle and 'henloo i am grate ElecTriciAn, I com to fix ya wires' is strong here) and proof of what I will be charged with before the team leaves. I have pre warned that the entire maintenance will be recorded (is not prohibited in the contract). The government (free) Advocate for the Citizen, has told me there is not much else I can do for now. oof.... ------------------------------------------------ AS I WAS WRITING THIS POST : I am called by the Advocate for the Citizen and they called the DEDDYE (department for maintenance of the system for DEI) and I will not be charged for ANYTHING, because : 1. DEDDYE never checked the box inthe time the contract was in my name 2.they never did the -free, their responsibility- change from analog to automatic uuuuhm counter? dunno the word. 3.they have never been in the area to check for seals 4.the box has a high voltage thing in it when the box is for low voltage usage/low voltage contract etc, the high voltage goe to nowhere, it just is there. 5.i could file for being endangered by no maintenance since I am legally disabled and (dunno) not as able as normal citizen to (dunno, look at the box from afar?), (dont care, getting free new electricity box with new automatic whatever) 6.something about exposed/naked wires is 'not my -the contract holder- issue to be charged with' I was advised to keep ALL AND ANY papers, online translations and get a transcript from the phone company (somehow I can, I checked.... lmao?!!???) And since this is Greece, to be prepared for the initial costs nonetheless, EVEN if I was told 'all free and shits'. ------------------------------------------------ AFTER WRITING THE LAST BIT : I am called by the local DEDDYE team. A frightened, agitated man, audibly trying to be formal and precise, talks in formal Greek wording to me. He talks reading from a list or a notepad: They will come as soon as possible, there will be no charge and I am off course allowed to record and stand by. They told me to NOT go near the box in a radius of 1,5 meter, especially if it rains or there is humidity. They dont understand how I still have electricity in the house. (well Mr. whoever...i dont either, must be Magic or the snails in the box) ---------------- This is it for now. I will edit/update this post about the Greek wasteland of electricity maintainable and the paperwork. PRAY to any and all gods/demons/Skyrim deities, DnD patrons and/or infernal creatures (my bae Cthulhu included) for me that I will not be charged after all. I have zero wish to hobble with my cane through legal offices and brandish papertrails....
item.dc:creator: Starcrested

item.title: Whoa
item.description: Thanks @MadameTiffany for the link to this gorgeous creature's Insta https://www.instagram.com/richie_the_mainecoon_/
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Terrified
item.description: A few weeks ago I had an MRI for something different that found this. A 2cm abnormal mass on my cerebellum. Neurosurgeon isn't sure what it is yet and I'm getting another MRI today. I'm a super hard stick on account of having hereditarily bad veins. So, I hate needles. Wife doesn't deal with stress. At all. So, I'm doing this all alone. Anyways, I just needed to vent a little bit. Gotta keep going because that's all I can do.
item.dc:creator: OldPenguinDoug

item.title: I GOT MARRIED
item.description: If you saw my previous posts you may have remembered the date but me and Nora got married April 20th!!! Luckily nothing went wrong and my parents didn't try to break in (they got banned for homophobia) I'm so bloody happy!!!!
